name: Bengal Subah

colors.symbolism: the Lion and Sun device inherited from the Mughal Alam

description: A green field bearing a gold lion and rising sun, a nautical standard of the Bengal Subah derived from the Mughal imperial Alam.
history: Bengal Subah was the largest province (subah) of the Mughal Empire. After 1717 it became the de facto independent domain of the hereditary Nawabs of Bengal under nominal Mughal suzerainty, until the British East India Company gained control following Plassey (1757).
